title: Untitled
research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review time-consuming, tedious, and error-prone, creating a need for automated systems that can generate literature reviews from PDF inputs.
method_summary: Three NLP approaches are implemented and compared: (1) Frequency-based spaCy model using word frequency calculation for sentence extraction, (2) Transformer-based Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset, (3) LLM-based GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). All approaches take PDF inputs and generate literature review summaries.
tasks: Automated literature review generation from PDF files; Multi-document summarization for academic papers
datasets: SciTLDR dataset (5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 scientific papers from Hugging Face)
claim_text: The LLM-based approach (GPT-3.5-turbo) achieved the highest ROUGE-1 score of 0.364, outperforming both T5 (0.268) and spaCy (0.257)
limitations: The model is not yet fully capable of completely automating literature reviews and still requires human reviewers for validation | The system was only evaluated on the SciTLDR dataset which may not represent all scientific domains
notes: The research presents three end-to-end pipelines for literature review automation, allowing users to generate literature reviews directly from PDF inputs. The LLM approach uses RAG with GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 as the backend. The T5 model was fine-tuned on SciTLDR while spaCy used extractive summarization based on word frequency. Evaluation was performed using ROUGE metrics on test data.

Evaluation was performed using ROUGE metrics on test data.

title: Untitled
research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review time-consuming, tedious, and error-prone, creating a need for automated systems that can generate literature reviews from PDF inputs.
method_summary: Three NLP approaches are implemented and compared: (1) Frequency-based spaCy model using word frequency calculation for sentence extraction, (2) Transformer-based Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset, (3) LLM-based GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). All approaches take PDF inputs and generate literature review summaries.
tasks: Automated literature review generation from PDF files; Multi-document summarization for academic papers
datasets: SciTLDR dataset (5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 scientific papers from Hugging Face)
claim_text: A functional GUI tool was successfully created using the best-performing LLM-based system where users can upload multiple PDFs to automatically generate coherent literature reviews
limitations: The model is not yet fully capable of completely automating literature reviews and still requires human reviewers for validation | The system was only evaluated on the SciTLDR dataset which may not represent all scientific domains
notes: The research presents three end-to-end pipelines for literature review automation, allowing users to generate literature reviews directly from PDF inputs. The LLM approach uses RAG with GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 as the backend. The T5 model was fine-tuned on SciTLDR while spaCy used extractive summarization based on word frequency. Evaluation was performed using ROUGE metrics on test data.
